picks.analysis: MSFT scores +16 — a Strong Call recommendation. The bullish read is led by Fundamentals (+7), reinforced by Narrative (+7). The biggest contributors are Positive Catalyst (+3, bullish), Guidance (+2, +23.3%), Sector Narrative (+2, tailwind). Cutting the other way: Support/Resistance (-1, ±0.0% to R), Short Interest % (-1, 1.0%). Within Technology we gave MSFT a +16 and DELL a +15 — same direction, lower conviction — which is why we'd take MSFT calls over DELL calls, mainly on Fundamentals (+7 vs +5) and Technicals (+1 vs +0). The suggested calls sit ~6.6% OTM with 48d to expiry, delta 0.30, mid $7.70 — well inside the 5-30% OTM / Δ 0.20-0.40 / ≤$35 premium / standard-monthly criteria.

reasons.fundamental: A guidance cut or a macro risk-off rotation is what usually drives price here — exit, don't average down.
reasons.mechanical: A surge in put buying / put-call pushing back above ~1.25 confirms the turn.
reasons.narrative: If "Aerospace & Defense Spending" flips against the trade, the reason for the position is gone — exit on the narrative, not the chart.

levels.prose: A close below 20-day support breaks the structure the trade is built on.
levels.proseAi: Cut the position if a daily close below 20-day support invalidates the structure.

triggers: Exit before earnings (2026-07-22): the post-report IV crush can erase a long premium even when the direction is right.
Time stop: don't carry into the final week before Sep 17 '26 — theta accelerates and the breakeven move gets harder each day.
Thesis check: if a higher-scoring setup appears or the original reason for the trade stops being true, rotate out regardless of price.
exitPlan.overviewAi: Ride the momentum to $400, taking profit incrementally as resistance is met, and cut losses if support breaks.

picks.analysis: GD scores +16 — a Strong Call recommendation. The bullish read is led by Fundamentals (+7), reinforced by Narrative (+6). The biggest contributors are Positive Catalyst (+3, bullish), Guidance (+2, +7.7%), Streak (+2, 3d green). Cutting the other way: Support/Resistance (-1, ±1.9% to R), Short Interest % (-1, 1.1%). Within Industrials we gave GD a +16 and FDX a +12 — same direction, lower conviction — which is why we'd take GD calls over FDX calls, mainly on Narrative (+6 vs +2) and Technicals (+3 vs +2). The suggested calls sit ~9.6% OTM with 111d to expiry, delta 0.30, mid $6.85 — well inside the 5-30% OTM / Δ 0.20-0.40 / ≤$35 premium / standard-monthly criteria.
